step_by_step_reasoning: Data scientists ingest genomics data into ad-hoc EC2 instances, which write raw data to FSx. Pipeline software is versioned and stored in ECR. EKS pulls these container images, mounts FSx to process raw data, and outputs processed matrices to an S3-based Data Lake. Finally, downstream data scientists and biologists analyze the curated data.

graph.name: Reproducible Genomics Data Curation Pipeline
graph.notes: Uses EKS and ECR to achieve reproducible science by versioning analytical pipelines. Leverages FSx for high-throughput genomic data curation, scaling from 0 to hundreds of nodes dynamically.

nodes.name: Data Engineers & Scientists
nodes.notes: Ingest hundreds to thousands of genomic samples.

nodes.name: Ad-hoc VMs
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Ad-hoc instances used to ingest raw genomics data.

nodes.name: FSx Storage
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores raw ATCG genomics sequence data, mounted to processing nodes.

nodes.name: Elastic Container Registry
nodes.notes: Stores frozen, versioned container images for pipeline reproducibility.

nodes.name: Elastic Kubernetes Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Auto-scales from 0 to hundreds of nodes to process and curate data.

nodes.name: Data Lake
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores processed and curated genomic matrices.

nodes.name: Biologists & Data Scientists
nodes.notes: Perform statistical analyses and target gene discovery.

edges.notes: Mount FSx storage to access raw data.
Persist curated matrices to Data Lake.
